results.compiled_code:
-- Singular test: returns rows when duplicates exist in the fact table.
-- dbt fails this test when any rows are returned.
-- A duplicate: same company + normalised_title + scrape_date.

select
    lower(company_name)     as company,
    normalised_title,
    scrape_date,
    count(*)                as duplicate_count
from "applyluma"."analytics"."fct_job_postings"
group by 1, 2, 3
having count(*) > 1

select breakdown_type
from "applyluma"."analytics"."agg_salary_insights"
where breakdown_type is null

select company_id
from "applyluma"."analytics"."dim_companies"
where company_id is null

select breakdown_value
from "applyluma"."analytics"."agg_salary_insights"
where breakdown_value is null

with all_values as (

    select
        job_source as value_field,
        count(*) as n_records

    from "applyluma"."analytics"."fct_job_postings"
    group by job_source

)

select *
from all_values
where value_field not in (
    'remotive','the_muse'
)

select job_count
from "applyluma"."analytics"."agg_salary_insights"
where job_count is null

with all_values as (

    select
        breakdown_type as value_field,
        count(*) as n_records

    from "applyluma"."analytics"."agg_salary_insights"
    group by breakdown_type

)

select *
from all_values
where value_field not in (
    'skill','location','company','employment_type'
)

with all_values as (

    select
        job_source as value_field,
        count(*) as n_records

    from "applyluma"."analytics"."stg_job_postings"
    group by job_source

)

select *
from all_values
where value_field not in (
    'remotive','the_muse'
)

with __dbt__cte__int_jobs_deduplicated as (
-- Two-pass dedup:
--   Pass 1 (Airflow): rows with is_duplicate = true are dropped here.
--   Pass 2 (dbt): same company + normalised title → keep the newest record.
-- Normalisation strips common seniority tokens so "Senior Python Dev"
-- and "Python Dev" collapse to the same key.

with staged as (
    select * from "applyluma"."analytics"."stg_job_postings"
),

normalised as (
    select
        *,
        lower(
            trim(
                regexp_replace(
                    regexp_replace(
                        job_title,
                        '\s*(senior|sr\.?|junior|jr\.?|lead|staff|principal|associate|mid-level|entry.level|intern)\s*',
                        ' ', 'gi'
                    ),
                    '\s{2,}', ' ', 'g'
                )
            )
        ) as normalised_title
    from staged
    where not is_duplicate
),

ranked as (
    select
        *,
        row_number() over (
            partition by lower(company_name), normalised_title
            order by scraped_at desc
        ) as dedup_rank
    from normalised
)

select
    job_id,
    job_source,
    job_id_external,
    job_title,
    normalised_title,
    company_name,
    location,
    job_description,
    job_url,
    salary_min,
    salary_max,
    employment_type,
    remote_allowed,
    extracted_skills,
    raw_data,
    scraped_at,
    created_at,
    scrape_date
from ranked
where dedup_rank = 1
) select normalised_title
from __dbt__cte__int_jobs_deduplicated
where normalised_title is null

with __dbt__cte__int_jobs_deduplicated as (
-- Two-pass dedup:
--   Pass 1 (Airflow): rows with is_duplicate = true are dropped here.
--   Pass 2 (dbt): same company + normalised title → keep the newest record.
-- Normalisation strips common seniority tokens so "Senior Python Dev"
-- and "Python Dev" collapse to the same key.

with staged as (
    select * from "applyluma"."analytics"."stg_job_postings"
),

normalised as (
    select
        *,
        lower(
            trim(
                regexp_replace(
                    regexp_replace(
                        job_title,
                        '\s*(senior|sr\.?|junior|jr\.?|lead|staff|principal|associate|mid-level|entry.level|intern)\s*',
                        ' ', 'gi'
                    ),
                    '\s{2,}', ' ', 'g'
                )
            )
        ) as normalised_title
    from staged
    where not is_duplicate
),

ranked as (
    select
        *,
        row_number() over (
            partition by lower(company_name), normalised_title
            order by scraped_at desc
        ) as dedup_rank
    from normalised
)

select
    job_id,
    job_source,
    job_id_external,
    job_title,
    normalised_title,
    company_name,
    location,
    job_description,
    job_url,
    salary_min,
    salary_max,
    employment_type,
    remote_allowed,
    extracted_skills,
    raw_data,
    scraped_at,
    created_at,
    scrape_date
from ranked
where dedup_rank = 1
),  __dbt__cte__int_jobs_with_skills as (
-- Enrich deduplicated jobs with a parsed skill array and skill count.
-- The extract_skills macro is called once in a CTE so the expensive
-- regex block runs exactly once per row.

with deduped as (
    select * from __dbt__cte__int_jobs_deduplicated
),

skills_extracted as (
    select
        *,
        case
            when jsonb_typeof(extracted_skills) = 'array'
                 and jsonb_array_length(extracted_skills) > 0
                then (
                    select array_agg(lower(trim(skill_text)))
                    from jsonb_array_elements_text(extracted_skills) as extracted(skill_text)
                    where nullif(trim(skill_text), '') is not null
                )
            else array_remove(ARRAY[
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bpython\b'                       then 'python'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bjavascript\b'                   then 'javascript'       end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\btypescript\b'                   then 'typescript'       end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bjava\b'                         then 'java'             end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bgolang\b|\bgo lang\b'           then 'golang'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\brust\b'                         then 'rust'             end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bfastapi\b'                      then 'fastapi'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\breact\.?js\b|\breact\b'         then 'react'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bvue\.?js\b|\bvue\b'             then 'vue'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bangular\b'                      then 'angular'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bdjango\b'                       then 'django'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bflask\b'                        then 'flask'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bnode\.?js\b'                    then 'node.js'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bpostgresql\b|\bpostgres\b'      then 'postgresql'       end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bmongodb\b|\bmongo\b'            then 'mongodb'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bmysql\b'                        then 'mysql'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bredis\b'                        then 'redis'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\belasticsearch\b'                then 'elasticsearch'    end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\baws\b|\bamazon web services\b'  then 'aws'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bgcp\b|\bgoogle cloud\b'         then 'gcp'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bazure\b|\bmicrosoft azure\b'    then 'azure'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bdocker\b'                       then 'docker'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bkubernetes\b|\bk8s\b'           then 'kubernetes'       end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bgraphql\b'                      then 'graphql'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bterraform\b'                    then 'terraform'        end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bgit\b'                          then 'git'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bci/?cd\b'                       then 'ci/cd'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bairflow\b'                      then 'airflow'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bdbt\b'                          then 'dbt'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bkafka\b'                        then 'kafka'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\brabbitmq\b'                     then 'rabbitmq'         end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bcelery\b'                       then 'celery'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bmachine learning\b|\bml\b'      then 'machine-learning' end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bllm\b|\blarge language model\b' then 'llm'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bopenai\b'                       then 'openai'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\blangchain\b'                    then 'langchain'        end
], null)

        end as skill_array
    from deduped
)

select
    *,
    coalesce(array_length(skill_array, 1), 0) as skill_count
from skills_extracted
) select job_id
from __dbt__cte__int_jobs_with_skills
where job_id is null

from __dbt__cte__int_jobs_deduplicated

with child as (
    select company_id as from_field
    from "applyluma"."analytics"."fct_job_postings"
    where company_id is not null
),

parent as (
    select company_id as to_field
    from "applyluma"."analytics"."dim_companies"
)

select
    from_field

from child
left join parent
    on child.from_field = parent.to_field

where parent.to_field is null

with all_values as (

    select
        source as value_field,
        count(*) as n_records

    from "applyluma"."public"."raw_job_postings"
    group by source

)

select *
from all_values
where value_field not in (
    'remotive','the_muse'
)

select company
from "applyluma"."public"."raw_job_postings"
where company is null

from "applyluma"."public"."raw_job_postings"
where description is null

from "applyluma"."public"."raw_job_postings"
where id is null

where scraped_at is null

select source

where source is null

where title is null

select
    id as unique_field,
    count(*) as n_records

from "applyluma"."public"."raw_job_postings"
where id is not null
group by id
having count(*) > 1

select url
from "applyluma"."public"."raw_job_postings"
where url is null

with staged as (
    select * from "applyluma"."analytics"."stg_job_postings"
),

normalised as (
    select
        *,
        lower(
            trim(
                regexp_replace(
                    regexp_replace(
                        job_title,
                        '\s*(senior|sr\.?|junior|jr\.?|lead|staff|principal|associate|mid-level|entry.level|intern)\s*',
                        ' ', 'gi'
                    ),
                    '\s{2,}', ' ', 'g'
                )
            )
        ) as normalised_title
    from staged
    where not is_duplicate
),

ranked as (
    select
        *,
        row_number() over (
            partition by lower(company_name), normalised_title
            order by scraped_at desc
        ) as dedup_rank
    from normalised
)

select
    job_id,
    job_source,
    job_id_external,
    job_title,
    normalised_title,
    company_name,
    location,
    job_description,
    job_url,
    salary_min,
    salary_max,
    employment_type,
    remote_allowed,
    extracted_skills,
    raw_data,
    scraped_at,
    created_at,
    scrape_date
from ranked
where dedup_rank = 1
),  __dbt__cte__int_jobs_with_skills as (
-- Enrich deduplicated jobs with a parsed skill array and skill count.
-- The extract_skills macro is called once in a CTE so the expensive
-- regex block runs exactly once per row.

with deduped as (
    select * from __dbt__cte__int_jobs_deduplicated
),

skills_extracted as (
    select
        *,
        case
            when jsonb_typeof(extracted_skills) = 'array'
                 and jsonb_array_length(extracted_skills) > 0
                then (
                    select array_agg(lower(trim(skill_text)))
                    from jsonb_array_elements_text(extracted_skills) as extracted(skill_text)
                    where nullif(trim(skill_text), '') is not null
                )
            else array_remove(ARRAY[
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bpython\b'                       then 'python'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bjavascript\b'                   then 'javascript'       end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\btypescript\b'                   then 'typescript'       end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bjava\b'                         then 'java'             end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bgolang\b|\bgo lang\b'           then 'golang'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\brust\b'                         then 'rust'             end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bfastapi\b'                      then 'fastapi'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\breact\.?js\b|\breact\b'         then 'react'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bvue\.?js\b|\bvue\b'             then 'vue'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bangular\b'                      then 'angular'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bdjango\b'                       then 'django'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bflask\b'                        then 'flask'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bnode\.?js\b'                    then 'node.js'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bpostgresql\b|\bpostgres\b'      then 'postgresql'       end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bmongodb\b|\bmongo\b'            then 'mongodb'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bmysql\b'                        then 'mysql'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bredis\b'                        then 'redis'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\belasticsearch\b'                then 'elasticsearch'    end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\baws\b|\bamazon web services\b'  then 'aws'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bgcp\b|\bgoogle cloud\b'         then 'gcp'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bazure\b|\bmicrosoft azure\b'    then 'azure'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bdocker\b'                       then 'docker'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bkubernetes\b|\bk8s\b'           then 'kubernetes'       end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bgraphql\b'                      then 'graphql'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bterraform\b'                    then 'terraform'        end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bgit\b'                          then 'git'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bci/?cd\b'                       then 'ci/cd'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bairflow\b'                      then 'airflow'          end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bdbt\b'                          then 'dbt'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bkafka\b'                        then 'kafka'            end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\brabbitmq\b'                     then 'rabbitmq'         end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bcelery\b'                       then 'celery'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bmachine learning\b|\bml\b'      then 'machine-learning' end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bllm\b|\blarge language model\b' then 'llm'              end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\bopenai\b'                       then 'openai'           end,
    case when lower(job_description) ~ '\blangchain\b'                    then 'langchain'        end
], null)

        end as skill_array
    from deduped
)

select
    *,
    coalesce(array_length(skill_array, 1), 0) as skill_count
from skills_extracted
) select
    job_id as unique_field,
    count(*) as n_records

from __dbt__cte__int_jobs_with_skills
where job_id is not null
group by job_id
having count(*) > 1
